description: Next-generation web dashboard for OpenStack with modern responsive UI, faster load times, and enhanced responsiveness. Extends beyond Horizon (sys-132) with Skyline-specific features. Rackspace-maintained fork at github.com/skyline-rxt. SAML Federation support via HTTP Routes with cookie-driven session persistence. rackspacecloud.com/release-notes (SAML/Shibboleth integration), github.com/skyline-rxt.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: Minimal persistent state — session data via cookies; backend in MariaDB
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for HTTPS; SAML assertions over TLS; cookie-driven session persistence via HTTP Routes
encryption_profile.key_management: Session key management; SAML/Shibboleth federation certificate management

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multiple Skyline pods behind K8s ingress with cookie-based session affinity via HTTP Routes

observability_stack.logging_coverage: Skyline API and console access logs, SAML authentication events
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: HTTP response codes, authentication success/failure rates, page load performance
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: SAML federation failures, HTTP 5xx rates, pod health

support_model.escalation_path: Genestack L1 → Skyline fork maintainers (github.com/skyline-rxt) → upstream community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: Skyline outage removes the primary next-gen web management interface for OpenStack. Customers fall back to Horizon (sys-132) or CLI/API access. SAML-federated users may lose web dashboard access entirely if Horizon does not support their federation configuration.

access_control_model: RBAC — OpenStack Keystone policy; SAML federation maps external IdP attributes to Keystone roles/projects

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from internet-facing web UI with SAML federation — primary customer-facing OpenStack management interface

backup_status.retention_period: N/A — stateless web frontend with cookie sessions

name: OpenStack Octavia Load Balancer
description: Scalable and automated load balancing service for OpenStack cloud applications. Supports Amphora driver (default) with haproxy backends. OVN load balancer option available as tech preview. Skyline UI currently limits to Amphora type. rackspacecloud.com/release-notes (OVN LB tech preview note).

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: Amphora VM images in Glance; load balancer config in MariaDB/Galera
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for Octavia API; TLS termination on load balancers; haproxy backend connections configurable
encryption_profile.key_management: Barbican integration for TLS certificate management on load balancers

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multiple Octavia API replicas; Amphora VMs support active-standby HA for individual LBs

observability_stack.logging_coverage: Octavia API, worker, health-manager, and housekeeping logs
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: Load balancer provisioning rates, Amphora health, API latency
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Amphora failover events, LB provisioning failures, health monitor alerts

support_model.escalation_path: Genestack L1 → OpenStack SME → upstream community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: Octavia outage prevents creation/modification of cloud load balancers. Existing load balancers continue operating (Amphora VMs run independently) but cannot be updated, scaled, or failover-managed. Customer applications relying on LBaaS for traffic distribution affected for new deployments.

access_control_model: RBAC — OpenStack Keystone policy; project-scoped LB ownership with admin/member roles

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from load balancer role — Octavia Amphora VMs run haproxy instances processing customer traffic

name: OpenStack Barbican Key Management
description: Secret storage and key management service for Genestack OpenStack. Helm chart 2024.1 (Caracal). Manages encryption keys, certificates, and passwords for OpenStack services. Release notes document barbican reinstall procedure and RabbitMQ queue configuration. rackspacecloud.com/openstack-barbican, docs.rackspacecloud.com/release-notes.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: Secrets encrypted in Barbican backend store (database or PKCS#11 HSM); MariaDB/Galera with database-level encryption
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for Barbican API; inter-service communication over TLS
encryption_profile.key_management: Barbican IS the key management service — master KEK protects all stored secrets; backend options include simple crypto, PKCS#11 HSM, or Vault

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multiple Barbican API and worker replicas behind K8s service; backend DB on Galera cluster

observability_stack.logging_coverage: Barbican API access logs, secret creation/retrieval events, key rotation events
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: API latency, secret store operations, certificate expiration tracking
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: API failures, secret store connectivity, certificate expiration warnings

support_model.escalation_path: Genestack L1 → OpenStack Security SME → upstream community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: Barbican outage prevents all secret retrieval, key creation, and certificate management operations. Services depending on Barbican for encryption keys (Cinder volume encryption, Nova ephemeral encryption, Octavia TLS termination) cannot perform key operations. Existing encrypted resources continue functioning but new encryption operations fail.

access_control_model: RBAC — OpenStack Keystone policy; project-scoped secret ownership; ACL-based sharing between projects; admin vs member vs reader roles

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from key management role — Barbican stores all encryption keys, certificates, and secrets for the entire OpenStack environment

backup_status.retention_period: Per MariaDB backup policy — secrets backup requires master KEK backup

name: MariaDB Galera Cluster (OpenStack)
description: Synchronous multi-master database cluster for all OpenStack services in Genestack. Managed by MariaDB Operator. Provides HA database backend for Keystone, Nova, Neutron, Cinder, Glance, Heat, and all other OpenStack service databases. rackspacecloud.com/infrastructure-mariadb, docs.rackspacecloud.com/genestack-structure-and-files.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: MariaDB data-at-rest encryption (InnoDB tablespace encryption) — available but enablement status requires confirmation
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for Galera replication (SST/IST); TLS for client connections from OpenStack services
encryption_profile.key_management: MariaDB file-based key management or encryption plugin; master key rotation support

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multi-master Galera cluster (minimum 3 nodes) with synchronous replication; quorum-based split-brain protection

observability_stack.logging_coverage: MariaDB error log, slow query log, Galera replication state changes
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: Galera cluster size, replication lag, wsrep flow control, query throughput, connection pool utilization
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Galera node departure, split-brain detection, replication lag thresholds, disk space alerts

support_model.escalation_path: Genestack L1 → DBA/MariaDB SME → MariaDB community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: MariaDB Galera cluster failure is a TOTAL OUTAGE of ALL OpenStack services. Every OpenStack service (Keystone, Nova, Neutron, Cinder, Glance, Heat, Barbican, Octavia, etc.) depends on MariaDB for state persistence. Complete loss of database quorum means zero cloud operations possible.

access_control_model: MySQL GRANT-based privilege system; per-service database user with schema-level isolation; root access restricted to MariaDB Operator

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from central database role — MariaDB Galera stores ALL OpenStack service state including Keystone credentials, Barbican master KEK, and every service database

name: RabbitMQ Cluster (OpenStack)
description: Message broker cluster required by most OpenStack services in Genestack for asynchronous communication. Managed by RabbitMQ Operator with Helm chart deployment. Critical dependency for Nova, Neutron, Cinder, Heat, and other OpenStack services. rackspacecloud.com/genestack-structure-and-files.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: RabbitMQ persistent message store on K8s PVCs; encryption depends on underlying storage backend
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for AMQP connections from OpenStack services; TLS for inter-node Erlang distribution
encryption_profile.key_management: TLS certificate management via K8s secrets; Erlang cookie for cluster authentication

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multi-node RabbitMQ cluster with quorum queues; managed by RabbitMQ Operator for automated recovery

observability_stack.logging_coverage: RabbitMQ server logs, connection events, queue operations
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: Queue depth, message rates, connection counts, memory/disk usage, cluster partition status
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Queue depth thresholds, cluster partition, memory alarm, disk alarm, unacknowledged message buildup

support_model.escalation_path: Genestack L1 → Messaging SME → RabbitMQ community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: RabbitMQ cluster failure causes TOTAL OUTAGE of ALL OpenStack asynchronous operations. Nova cannot schedule VMs, Neutron agents cannot receive network configuration, Cinder cannot process volume operations, Heat stacks stall. Equivalent in severity to MariaDB Galera failure — all cloud operations halt.

access_control_model: RabbitMQ vhost-based isolation; per-service user with topic/queue permissions; management UI restricted to admin users

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from message broker role — RabbitMQ carries ALL inter-service communication for OpenStack including Nova scheduling, Neutron agent updates, and Cinder operations

backup_status.retention_period: N/A — message broker; transient data; cluster state recovers via quorum

name: cert-manager TLS Certificate Management
description: Kubernetes-native certificate management controller for Genestack. Release notes reference cert-manager's ExperimentalGatewayAPISupport option. Automates TLS certificate issuance and renewal for OpenStack service endpoints exposed via Envoy Gateway API. rackspacecloud.com/release-notes.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: TLS private keys stored as K8s Secrets (etcd encryption dependency)
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: cert-manager controller communicates with ACME/CA endpoints over TLS; webhook serves over TLS
encryption_profile.key_management: Automated certificate lifecycle management; private keys in K8s Secrets; CA issuer configuration per ClusterIssuer/Issuer CRD

availability_design.redundancy_level: Single active controller with leader election; K8s restart policy

support_model.escalation_path: SRE → Platform Engineering → Jetstack/cert-manager Community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: TLS certificate renewal halts across all Genestack services; existing certificates continue working until expiry; after expiry, cascading TLS failures across ALL OpenStack API endpoints exposed via Envoy (sys-182); SAML federation broken

access_control_model: Kubernetes RBAC - cert-manager controller service account with cluster-wide Secret read/write for certificate storage; ClusterIssuer/Issuer CRDs for policy

backup_status.retention_period: N/A - certificates and keys stored in K8s Secrets (etcd backup covers these)

name: Prometheus AlertManager
description: Alert routing and notification engine deployed as part of kube-prometheus-stack in Genestack. Handles AlertManagerConfig and PrometheusRule CRDs for dynamic alerting. Separate from the core Prometheus server (sys-095). rackspacecloud.com/monitoring-info.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: Alertmanager state (silences, notification log) stored in local PV; encryption depends on underlying storage provider
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: HTTP API for alert ingestion from Prometheus; webhook notifications to receivers (Slack, PagerDuty, email) over HTTPS
encryption_profile.key_management: Webhook receiver credentials and notification channel secrets stored in K8s Secrets

availability_design.redundancy_level: Alertmanager cluster with gossip protocol for HA deduplication

support_model.escalation_path: SRE → Platform Engineering → Prometheus Community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: All Genestack alert notifications cease; Prometheus continues collecting metrics but no alerts are routed to on-call teams; silent failures across entire platform; critical incidents go unnoticed until customer impact

access_control_model: Kubernetes RBAC for CRD management (AlertManagerConfig, PrometheusRule); Alertmanager API typically unauthenticated within cluster

backup_status.retention_period: N/A - silences and notification log are ephemeral; alert rules defined in PrometheusRule CRDs (Git-backed)

name: HashiCorp Vault Enterprise (Secrets Management)
description: Enterprise secrets management platform deployed across Genestack Kubernetes clusters and internal DevOps pipelines. Manages TLS certificates, API keys, database credentials, and PKI infrastructure. Integrated with Kubernetes via Vault Agent Injector and CSI driver. Used as secrets backend for OpenStack-Helm charts in Genestack deployment. Also serves as certificate authority for internal mTLS service mesh.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: AES-256-GCM (Vault seal/unseal with auto-unseal via Transit or cloud KMS)
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.3 (API), mTLS (cluster replication)
encryption_profile.key_management: Internal barrier keyring + external auto-unseal (Transit, AWS KMS, or Azure Key Vault)

availability_design.redundancy_level: Raft HA with 3-5 node consensus

observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Seal status, leader changes, token expiry, audit failures

support_model.escalation_path: HashiCorp Support Portal → TAM → Engineering

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: Complete halt of secrets rotation, certificate issuance, and credential vending for all Genestack services and DevOps pipelines. No new deployments possible. Existing services degrade as secrets/certs expire.

access_control_model: Policy-based ACL (HCL policies) + Sentinel EGP/RGP (Enterprise)

provenance.primary_data_source: HashiCorp Vault Helm chart referenced in Rackspace Genestack documentation at rackerlabs/genestack on GitHub
